issues.criterion: non_goals: "No creation or edit of any other ecosystem/*.md protocol document (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, 02_evidence_baseline.md, 03_engineering_audit.md, 04-08) -- those belong to features 034, 035, 036, and successor-phase-deferred protocols", verified by verification entry 14 ("no other ecosystem/*.md protocol document (00/02-08) was created").
issues.evidence: Entry 14's command is `! ls ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || {...; exit 1;}`. This passes a single multi-argument `ls` invocation listing all 8 forbidden filenames. Reproduced live, twice: (1) baseline (none of the 8 exist) -- `ls <8 files>` exits 2 (not-found), so `! ls ...` exits 0 -> PASS, as intended. (2) Created ONE throwaway out-of-scope file, `ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md` (touch), and re-ran the exact same multi-arg `ls` invocation: it STILL exits 2, because 7 of the 8 listed files are still missing -- GNU `ls`'s exit code with multiple arguments reflects 'was ANY argument missing', not 'were ALL arguments missing'. So `! ls ...` is STILL 0 -> the full entry-14 command still prints PASS even though a genuine, forbidden ecosystem protocol document now exists in the tree. Independently re-verified the mechanism using the LITERAL command string extracted programmatically from .agent/contracts/033.json (not retyped) via a path-neutral before/after comparison, and separately confirmed via a raw `ls`-exit-code check (`ls exit=2` in both the zero-forbidden-files case and the one-forbidden-file case). The probe file was removed immediately after (`rm -f ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md`); `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- ecosystem/` is empty, confirming no residue. This is the same class of defect the evaluator rejected contract 031 rev0 for (a verification command that cannot distinguish a compliant state from a violating one) -- here entry 14 would only ever correctly fail if a generator created ALL 8 forbidden documents simultaneously, an practically-impossible bar, and gives a false PASS for the realistic single-document (or up-to-seven-document) violation the non-goal is meant to catch.
issues.required_fix: Replace the single multi-argument `ls` call with a per-file existence loop (or 8 independent `test -e`/`[ -e ... ]` checks ORed together), each of which fails the whole check if that ONE file exists, e.g.: `for f in ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md; do if [ -e "$f" ]; then echo "out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document created: $f"; exit 1; fi; done; echo PASS`. Re-verify with the same single-file-created probe used in this review (create exactly one of the 8, confirm the fixed check now correctly fails; remove it, confirm PASS returns) before re-review.

observations: SCHEMA VALIDITY: `python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/contracts/033.json')), json.load(open('schema/contract.schema.json')))"` re-run as part of this review: exits 0, no error. Contract 033 (revision 0) is schema-valid against schema/contract.schema.json.

ARRAY LENGTH: parsed .agent/contracts/033.json's 'verification' array programmatically: exactly 14 entries, matching evidence_convention's own stated count ('the verification array has exactly 14 entries') and the .agent/evidence/033-verify-01.txt .. -14.txt naming scheme it describes. No off-by-one.

ANTI-VACUITY MASTER PROOF (entry 2) RE-CONFIRMED: `git show HEAD:ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md` fails (file absent at HEAD 45e3b67); `ls ecosystem/` shows only README.md on disk right now. This independently confirms the target artifact does not exist anywhere in the tree at review time, so entries 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 (all content-anchored) are non-vacuous by construction, not merely by the contract's own say-so.

FULL 14-ENTRY BASELINE RE-RUN (all 14 commands extracted programmatically from the live 033.json, not retyped, executed from the repository root against the live pre-implementation tree): entries 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 (file-existence and content-anchored checks) correctly FAIL pre-implementation (file absent); entry 2 (anti-vacuity master proof) correctly PASSES; entries 9-14 (regression/scope/immutability/non-goal guards) all currently PASS on the clean pre-implementation tree, as expected for checks that are meant to hold both before and after a compliant implementation.

SECTION-ANCHORED CHECKS (entries 5-8, using tools/verify_lib.sh's vlib_section_grep) PROVEN GENUINELY DISCRIMINATING, not vacuous to a whole-document grep: built one GOOD scratch fixture (matching design_notes.planned_document_structure verbatim, outside the repo tree, never touching the contracted path) plus seven mutated BAD fixtures (missing 'exactly one' in Sec 3; missing 'MUST NOT proceed' in Sec 4; missing 'operator approval' in Sec 5; missing 'before execution continues' in Sec 5; missing the literal artifact path in Sec 6; a cross-section-leakage fixture where every required token/phrase is present SOMEWHERE in the document body but NOT in its correct numbered section; and a no-backticks fixture where the three verdict names appear as plain prose, not backtick-wrapped). Ran the LITERAL, programmatically-extracted (not retyped) commands for entries 5, 6, 7, 8 against each fixture via path substitution (contracted path string swapped for the scratch fixture's absolute path, executed with bash -c): each entry passes on GOOD, fails ONLY on the BAD fixture(s) targeting its own section/phrase, and passes on BAD fixtures targeting unrelated sections (e.g. entry 5 is unaffected by a Sec-4/Sec-5/Sec-6-only mutation). The cross-section-leakage fixture -- the most rigorous test, proving vlib_section_grep is not merely a whole-file grep in disguise -- correctly FAILS all four entries (5, 6, 7, 8) even though every required token/phrase string exists in the document's Sec 1 body text, because none of it falls within the awk-computed span of its required numbered heading. The no-backticks fixture correctly fails entry 5 and entry 7 (the two entries requiring backtick-wrapped verdict tokens), confirming the backtick-delimiter requirement is genuinely enforced, not superficial. All fixture files and probe artifacts live only under the session scratchpad, outside the repository tree; no repo file was created, and `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` is unchanged before/after this portion of the review.

FRONTMATTER CHECKS (entries 3, 4) PROVEN DISCRIMINATING: re-ran entry 3's logic against a fixture missing the schema-required 'description' key -- correctly raises jsonschema.ValidationError ('description' is a required property); against a fixture with status: proposed -- correctly raises ValidationError ('proposed' is not one of [draft, active, deprecated]), independently corroborating design_notes.planned_document_structure's own claim that 'proposed' is not a valid status value for this schema. Re-ran entry 4's logic against a fixture with id/status/authoritative_source all altered to incorrect values -- correctly fails; against the corrected GOOD fixture -- correctly passes.

SCOPE GUARD (entry 10) PROVEN LOAD-BEARING via a disposable, fully-reverted probe: created a throwaway untracked root-level file `bogus_033_probe.txt`, re-ran the literal `vlib_scope_guard` invocation from entry 10 -- correctly FAILS ('vlib_scope_guard: out-of-scope changed path: bogus_033_probe.txt'); removed the probe, re-ran -- correctly PASSES again. Confirms the concurrency-tolerant allow-list (ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/README.md, NIZAM.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) is exactly as narrow as claimed -- it tolerates the named paths and nothing else.

.AGENT/ IMMUTABILITY GUARD (entry 11) PROVEN LOAD-BEARING AND CORRECTLY CONCURRENCY-TOLERANT via three disposable, fully-reverted probes: (a) appended a trailing newline to the HISTORICAL `.agent/qa/030.json` -- guard correctly FAILS ('immutable audit record(s) touched: M .agent/qa/030.json'); reverted with `git checkout -- .agent/qa/030.json`, confirmed byte-identical restoration via `git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa/030.json`. (b) re-ran the guard immediately after revert -- correctly PASSES again. (c) created a throwaway `.agent/qa/032-contract-review.json` (simulating feature 032's own concurrently-authored qa artifact appearing in this shared tree) -- guard correctly continues to PASS (tolerated by the `:(exclude).agent/qa/032*.json` pathspec), confirming the contract's stated concurrency-tolerance for feature 032's own in-flight .agent artifacts is real, not merely asserted; probe removed afterward.

NON-GOAL SCHEMA/TOOLS BYTE-IDENTITY GUARD (entry 12) PROVEN LOAD-BEARING: appended a byte to `schema/manifest.schema.json`, re-ran entry 12's literal command -- correctly FAILS and names the modified file; reverted with `git checkout -- schema/manifest.schema.json`, re-ran -- correctly PASSES again.

NON-GOAL PLANNING-DOC BYTE-IDENTITY GUARD (entry 13) PROVEN LOAD-BEARING: appended a byte to `docs/planning/manifest.json`, re-ran entry 13's literal command -- correctly FAILS; reverted with `git checkout -- docs/planning/manifest.json`, re-ran -- correctly PASSES again.

ENTRY 9 (validate.sh regression guard) is a pure regression check, not meant to discriminate this feature's own implementation state (this docs-only feature is not expected to change C1-C11) -- confirmed currently green ('SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed') at baseline; consistent with the 031-precedent treatment of the equivalent entry as legitimate and non-blocking despite not being content-discriminating.

MINOR, NON-BLOCKING: design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts's prose says entry 12 is 'the .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard' -- this is actually entry 11 in the final array (off-by-one in the design_notes' own internal cross-reference, harmless, matching the same class of harmless narrative slip noted non-blocking in the 031-rev1 review ('11 vs 10')). Not a verification defect since the actual entry 11 command and its concurrency-tolerant exclusions are correct and independently confirmed above; recommend a documentation tidy-up in the next revision but does not block approval on its own.

MINOR, NON-BLOCKING: design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof says the anti-vacuity master proof (entry 2) covers 'entries 3-9'; entry 9 (validate.sh green) is a repo-wide regression check, not a content-anchored check keyed to the new file's existence, so the precise span it should describe is 'entries 3-8'. Same class of harmless prose imprecision as above; does not affect the actual verification logic.

CONCURRENCY: read .agent/contracts/032.json (Ecosystem Module Index, concurrently in flight in the same tree) to cross-check 033's concurrency_fence claims. Confirmed structurally consistent and non-conflicting: 032's own scope explicitly excludes ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (033's sole deliverable) from its files_modify/files_create and non_goals; 033's scope explicitly excludes ecosystem/README.md and NIZAM.json (032's files) from its own files_create/files_modify and non_goals; both contracts' entry-11/entry-12-equivalent .agent immutability guards symmetrically allow-list the other feature's 0NN.json contract and 0NN*.json qa/review artifacts. No edit was made to any 032 surface (ecosystem/README.md, NIZAM.json, .agent/contracts/032.json, any .agent/qa/032*.json, any .agent/evidence/032*) during this review; confirmed by the unchanged `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after.

REPOSITORY LEFT UNMODIFIED: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after this entire review shows the identical 3-path set (`.agent/contracts/032.json`, `.agent/contracts/033.json`, `v0.6.0-release-notes.md`, all untracked) and `git diff HEAD --stat` is empty. All scratch fixtures and probe artifacts (bogus_033_probe.txt, the .agent/qa/030.json edit, the throwaway .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json, the schema/manifest.schema.json byte, the docs/planning/manifest.json byte, the throwaway ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md) were created and removed/reverted within this same review session and left no residue. No edit was made to .agent/run_state.json.

.agent/validator/ currently holds Mode A files only through 030 (026-mode-a.json .. 030-mode-b.json); no 031/032/033-mode-a.json file exists on disk yet despite the task's statement that contract 033 was 'just approved by @validator Mode A'. This is an orchestrator/validator durable-state-recording gap (the Durable State Rule expects subagent results in .agent/, not oral tradition), not a defect in contract 033 itself; flagged for orchestrator awareness only, non-blocking for this contract-testability review.

final_verdict.summary: REJECTED at revision 0. Thirteen of the fourteen verification commands are sound, non-vacuous, and load-bearing -- independently re-run (not trusted from the contract's own claims) using the literal, programmatically-extracted command strings against both the live pre-implementation working tree (entries 1/3/4/5/6/7/8 correctly FAIL, since ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md does not exist anywhere in the tree or at HEAD; entries 2/9/10/11/12/13/14(as-authored) currently PASS) and a battery of disposable scratch fixtures and reverted live-tree mutation probes: the four section-anchored checks (entries 5-8, built on tools/verify_lib.sh's vlib_section_grep) were proven genuinely discriminating via GOOD/BAD fixture pairs INCLUDING a cross-section-leakage fixture where every required token/phrase appears in the document body but outside its required numbered section -- all four entries correctly failed that fixture, proving the checks are not a whole-file grep in disguise; the frontmatter checks (entries 3, 4) were proven discriminating against missing-required-key and invalid-enum-value fixtures; the scope guard (entry 10), the .agent/ immutability guard (entry 11, including its concurrency-tolerance for feature 032's own in-flight artifacts), and the two non-goal byte-identity guards (entries 12, 13) were each proven load-bearing via disposable, fully-reverted positive-control probes on the live tree. However, verification entry 14 (`! ls <8 forbidden ecosystem/*.md filenames> >/dev/null 2>&1`) is a genuine, reproduced false-pass: GNU ls's exit code with multiple file arguments reflects whether ANY argument was not found, not whether ALL were not found, so the command exits identically (nonzero -> the negated check PASSES) whether zero, one, or up to seven of the eight forbidden documents exist in the tree. Live reproduction: creating exactly one forbidden document (ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md) and re-running the literal entry-14 command still yields PASS. This is the same class of non-discriminating verification defect the evaluator rejected contract 031 rev0 for (verification entry 7's bare grep), and it means the non-goal 'no other ecosystem/*.md protocol document was created' currently has NO working verification -- a generator could create any single out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document during implementation and entry 14 would not catch it. Required before re-review: replace the multi-argument ls call with a per-file existence loop (or equivalent OR-of-independent-test-e checks) that fails as soon as ANY one of the 8 named files exists, and re-verify with the same single-file-creation probe used in this review. Two harmless, non-blocking prose imprecisions were also noted (an off-by-one in design_notes' internal entry-number cross-reference, and an 'entries 3-9' vs the more precise 'entries 3-8' span in the dry-run-proof narrative) -- neither affects the verification logic and neither blocks approval on its own. Not approved for implementation as-is; approve only after entry 14 is fixed and its fix is proven non-vacuous with a live single-document-creation probe.
